info.title: Gophish API (Yazamco SOC Edition)
info.description:
REST API for this Gophish instance, including all Yazamco-specific extensions: **Companies** (Hudu-backed organizations), **Domains** (n8n / Cloudflare / Microsoft 365 provisioning), **template rotation pools**, **per-recipient email subjects**, **resend** endpoints and the **spam-report** webhook trigger.

## Authentication
Every endpoint requires your personal API key, found under **Account Settings**. Supply it one of two ways:

- `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>` header (recommended)
- `?api_key=<API_KEY>` query parameter

## Permissions
- Users with the **user** role can manage only the objects they own; **admin** users see everything.
- Endpoints marked *admin only* require the `modify_system` permission (User Management, Webhooks).
- Accounts in *view-only* mode may only issue GET requests.

## Conventions
- All write endpoints accept and return `application/json` (except group CSV import, which is `multipart/form-data`).
- Dates are RFC 3339 / ISO-8601 with timezone, e.g. `2026-06-04T09:30:00+03:00`. The zero date `0001-01-01T00:00:00Z` means "not set".
- Errors return a `GenericResponse` envelope: `{"message": "...", "success": false, "data": null}`.

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Create, monitor, resend and complete phishing campaigns

tags.name: Results
tags.description: Per-recipient campaign results, resends and the spam-report webhook

tags.name: Companies
tags.description: Organizations campaigns are attributed to. Synced from Hudu via the n8n hudu-companies webhook

tags.name: Domains
tags.description: Sending / landing domains. Creating a domain dispatches an n8n webhook that provisions DNS (Cloudflare) and optionally Microsoft 365 records

tags.name: Templates
tags.description: Email templates, including attachments and template variables

tags.name: Landing Pages
tags.description: Phishing landing pages with optional credential capture

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User management (admin only for the collection endpoints)

tags.name: IMAP
tags.description: IMAP account used to detect user-reported phishing emails

tags.name: Import
tags.description: Helpers for importing groups (CSV), raw emails and cloning sites

tags.name: Utilities
tags.description: Test emails and API key reset

get.description: Returns all campaigns visible to the authenticated user (admins see every user's campaigns), including full results and timeline for each. For dashboards prefer `/campaigns/summary`, which is much lighter.

post.description:
Creates a new campaign. Referenced objects (template(s), page, groups, sending profile) are looked up **by name** and must already exist.

- If `launch_date` is omitted or in the past, the campaign launches **immediately**.
- If `launch_date` is in the future the campaign is queued (`status: Queued`) and the background worker launches it on time.
- If `send_by_date` is set, email sending is spread evenly between `launch_date` and `send_by_date`.
- **Template rotation (Yazamco extension):** provide multiple templates in `templates[]` and each recipient is randomly assigned one of them at launch. `template` may be omitted when `templates` is provided; the first pool entry is kept in `template` for compatibility.
- `company_id` attributes the campaign to a Company (used in reports and the spam-report webhook).

get.description: Lightweight overview of every visible campaign: status, dates, attributed company and aggregate stats. No per-recipient data.
Returns full campaign details including results and the event timeline.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the campaign, its results, events and queued emails. **Irreversible.**

get.description: Returns just the per-recipient results and event timeline — significantly less data than fetching the full campaign (no template/page/SMTP bodies).
Aggregate stats for a single campaign (sent / opened / clicked / submitted / reported / errors).
Marks the campaign as **Completed**. No further emails are sent; subsequent clicks on phishing links return a 404 page. (Note: this is a GET request for historical compatibility.)

post.description: Re-sends the phishing email to the selected recipients of a campaign. Each recipient keeps their originally-assigned template (rotation pools are respected). Invalid result IDs are skipped; the response reports how many emails were queued. Sending is asynchronous.

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The recipient's result ID (`Result.id`, e.g. `b7c9d2e`) — **not** a numeric row id.

post.summary: Resend to a single recipient (Yazamco extension)
post.description: Re-sends the phishing email to one recipient. The result must belong to the campaign. Sending is asynchronous.

parameters.name: rid
parameters.description: The recipient's result ID (`Result.id`, e.g. `b7c9d2e`) — **not** a numeric row id.

post.summary: Mark a recipient as reported (Yazamco extension)
post.description:
Manually flags a single recipient as having reported the phishing email. Sets the recipient's `reported` flag to `true` and adds an **Email Reported** event to the campaign timeline.

This is the authenticated, admin-facing counterpart to the automatic IMAP / report-button mechanism — use it to record a recipient who reported the email out-of-band (e.g. by phone or by forwarding it to the SOC).

The call is **idempotent**: if the recipient is already marked as reported, it returns `200` without adding a duplicate timeline event. The result must belong to the campaign.

200.description: Recipient marked as reported (or already was)

post.description:
Fires the n8n **spam-report** webhook for this campaign with the full per-recipient result set (including each recipient's rendered `email_subject`, assigned `template_id`, status and reported flag) plus the attributed Company (English + Hebrew names, Hudu customer id).

The webhook is **fire-and-forget**: a `200` here means the request was queued, not that n8n succeeded. Requires `spam_report_secret` in `config.json`; the payload is sent with the `x-techform-secret` header. Default endpoint: `https://n8n.yazamco.pro/webhook/gophish-check-spam-report-365` (override with `spam_report_url`).

500.description: Webhook not configured (`spam_report_secret` missing) or other failure

get.description: Returns all target groups visible to the user, including the full target list of each group. For large groups prefer `/groups/summary`.

post.description: Creates a target group. `name` must be unique per user and at least one target is required.

get.description: Returns each group with its target **count** instead of the full member list.

put.description: Replaces the group. The body **must** include `id` matching the path parameter, the `name`, and the **complete** target list (targets not present in the body are removed).

get.description: Returns the companies (organizations) visible to the user, sorted by name. Campaigns reference a company via `company_id`.

post.description: Creates a company. `name` must be unique per user. `name_he` (Hebrew display name, used in generated reports) and `customer_id` (Hudu customer id) are typically sourced from `/companies/hudu`.

get.summary: List companies known in Hudu (Yazamco extension)
get.description:
Proxies the n8n **hudu-companies** webhook server-side (the shared secret never reaches the browser) and returns the **non-archived** companies known in Hudu. Used by the "New Company" picker.

Requires `hudu_companies_secret` in `config.json`. Default endpoint: `https://n8n.yazamco.pro/webhook/hudu-companies` (override with `hudu_companies_url`). Note the field mapping: `nickname` = Hebrew name, `id_number` = Hudu customer id.

502.description: Hudu webhook unreachable, returned an error, or not configured (`hudu_companies_secret` missing)

put.description: The body must include `id` matching the path parameter.

delete.description: Deletes the company. Campaigns previously attributed to it are left unassigned (`company_id` reset to 0), not deleted.

get.description: Returns the sending/landing domains visible to the user, sorted by name.

post.description:
Creates a domain and dispatches a `domain.created` webhook to the configured n8n endpoint (signed with `X-Gophish-Signature`). n8n performs the actual Cloudflare DNS and Microsoft Graph work — Gophish itself provisions nothing.

- `name` must be unique per user.
- `status` defaults to `pending` when omitted.
- `configure_365: true` sets `m365_status` to `connected` and adds the Microsoft 365 mail record set (MX, SPF, autodiscover, DKIM selectors, DMARC) to the desired records.
- `auto_a_record: true` adds a root A record pointing at `ip`.
- The response includes `records[]` — the desired DNS record set that was sent to n8n (not persisted; shown so the UI can display it).

201.description: Domain created; `records` holds the desired DNS record set

put.description: The body must include `id` matching the path parameter. Setting `configure_365` recomputes `m365_status` (`connected` / `not_connected`). Updating does **not** re-dispatch the n8n webhook.

delete.description: Deletes the domain record in Gophish only — DNS records already provisioned by n8n are not touched.

post.description: Creates an email template. `name` must be unique per user and at least one of `text` / `html` is required. **Template variables** available in `subject`, `text` and `html`: `{{.FirstName}}`, `{{.LastName}}`, `{{.Position}}`, `{{.Email}}`, `{{.From}}`, `{{.URL}}` (phishing link incl. `?rid=`), `{{.TrackingURL}}`, `{{.Tracker}}` (`` open tracker), `{{.RId}}`, `{{.BaseURL}}`. To import an existing email use `/import/email` first and paste the result here.

put.description: The body must include `id` matching the path parameter and the complete template (attachments included — omitted attachments are removed).

post.description:
Creates a landing page. `name` must be unique per user.

On save, Gophish rewrites the HTML: all `<form>` actions are pointed back at the phishing server, and input `name` attributes are stripped/kept according to `capture_credentials` / `capture_passwords`. **Warning:** with `capture_passwords: true`, submitted passwords are stored in plaintext in the database.

`redirect_url` is where victims are sent after submitting the form (supports template variables). Use `/import/site` to clone an existing site first.

put.description: The body must include `id` matching the path parameter.

post.description: Creates an SMTP sending profile. `name` must be unique per user. `host` is `host:port` (port defaults to 25 when omitted). `from_address` must be a plain email address. Custom `headers` are added to every email sent with this profile (header values support template variables such as `{{.URL}}`).

put.description: The body must include `id` matching the path parameter and pass SMTP validation.

post.description: Creates a user account. The password must satisfy the password policy (minimum 8 characters). A fresh API key is generated automatically. Roles: `admin` (full access) or `user`.

get.description: Admins may fetch any user; other users may only fetch their own account.

put.description: Admins may modify any user; other users only themselves and may **not** change their own role. Password is optional — when provided it must satisfy the password policy. The last remaining admin cannot be demoted.

delete.description: Admins may delete any user; other users may only delete their own account.

get.description: Returns all outbound webhooks, active and disabled. Active webhooks receive a signed JSON POST (`X-Gophish-Signature: sha256=<hmac>`) for every campaign event (sent / opened / clicked / submitted / reported).

put.description: The `id` is taken from the path; any `id` in the body is ignored.

post.summary: Send a test ping to a webhook (admin only)
post.description: POSTs `{"success": true}` (signed) to the webhook's URL to verify it is reachable. Returns the webhook on success.

400.description: The remote endpoint rejected the ping or was unreachable

get.description: Returns the user's IMAP configuration as a list (empty list when not configured). The IMAP account is polled for user-reported phishing emails; matching campaign emails are marked **Email Reported**.

post.description: Creates or replaces the user's IMAP configuration. Note: `port` and `imap_freq` are JSON **strings** (e.g. `"993"`).

Attempts to log in with the supplied IMAP settings without saving them. **Note:** a failed login still returns HTTP 200 with `success: false` and the error message; a successful login returns HTTP 201.

Parses an uploaded CSV file and returns the targets it contains — it does **not** create a group; pass the result to `POST /groups/`.

Expected headers (case-insensitive, order-free): `First Name`, `Last Name`, `Email`, `Position`.

post.summary: Parse a raw email (.eml) into template content
post.description: Parses a raw RFC 2045 email (full headers + body) and returns its subject, text and HTML parts, ready to be saved as a template. With `convert_links: true`, every `<a href>` in the HTML is rewritten to `{{.URL}}`.

content.description: The full raw email source (headers + body)

convert_links.description: Rewrite all links in the HTML to `{{.URL}}`

post.description:
Fetches the given URL server-side and returns its HTML, prepared for use as a landing page:
- a `<base href>` tag is injected so relative resources keep loading from the original site
- every `<form>` gets a hidden `__original_url` input recording where it came from

TLS certificate errors on the target site are ignored. (`include_resources` is accepted but currently has no effect.)

Sends a single test email to the given recipient.

- `template.name` — optional; when omitted a built-in "It works!" message is sent. When set, the template is looked up by name.
- `smtp` — either reference an existing profile by `name`, or inline a **complete** sending profile (host + from_address etc.).
- `page.name` — optional landing page reference, used so `{{.URL}}` resolves.
- `url` — base phishing URL used to render `{{.URL}}` / `{{.TrackingURL}}` in the test.

The call **blocks** until the SMTP transaction finishes, so failures (bad credentials, connection refused) are reported synchronously.
Generates a fresh API key for the authenticated user and **invalidates the current one immediately**. The new key is returned in `data`.

BearerAuth.description: Your personal API key from Account Settings, sent as `Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>`.

ApiKeyQuery.name: api_key
ApiKeyQuery.description: Your personal API key as a query parameter, e.g. `?api_key=<API_KEY>`.

BadRequest.description: Validation failed or malformed JSON — see `message`

Forbidden.description: Insufficient permissions (admin / `modify_system` required, or view-only account attempting a write)

NotFound.description: Object not found (or not owned by you)

MethodNotAllowed.description: Wrong HTTP method for this endpoint

GenericResponse.description: Standard response envelope for status messages and errors.

data.description: Optional payload (e.g. the new API key on `/reset`)

Campaign.description: A phishing campaign with its full configuration, per-recipient results and event timeline.

launch_date.description: When sending starts. Omit / past value = launch immediately.

send_by_date.description: Optional deadline; sending is spread evenly between launch_date and this time. Zero date = send as fast as possible.

templates.description: **Yazamco extension** — optional template rotation pool. When more than one template is given, each recipient is randomly assigned one at launch; `template` holds the first pool entry for compatibility.

url.description: Base URL of the phishing server; `{{.URL}}` in templates resolves to this plus `?rid=<result id>`

company_id.description: ID of the Company this campaign is attributed to (0 = unassigned)

CampaignRequest.description: Payload for creating a campaign. Referenced objects are matched **by name** and must already exist.

template.description: Email template reference (by name). Optional when `templates` is provided.

templates.description: **Yazamco extension** — rotation pool of template references. Each recipient gets a random one.

url.description: Phishing server base URL used in `{{.URL}}`

send_by_date.description: Must be after launch_date when set

CampaignResults.description: Slim view of a campaign: results + timeline only.

CampaignStats.description: Aggregate counters. Each counter is the number of recipients who reached at least that stage (a recipient who submitted data also counts in `clicked`, `opened` and `sent`).

Result.description: A single recipient in a campaign and their current state.

id.description: The recipient's unique result id (`rid`) — appears as `?rid=` in phishing URLs and is used by the resend endpoints

send_date.description: When the email was sent (or is scheduled)

reported.description: Whether the recipient reported the email (via IMAP detection or the report endpoint)

template_id.description: **Yazamco extension** — the template assigned to this recipient from the rotation pool (0 = campaign's single template)

email_subject.description: **Yazamco extension** — the rendered subject line this recipient actually received (per-recipient, since subjects can contain template variables and campaigns can rotate templates)
email_subject.example: Action required: password expires today, Dana

ResultStatus.description: The recipient's furthest stage in the campaign.

Event.description: A timeline event for a campaign.

email.description: Recipient email (empty for campaign-level events like 'Campaign Created')

details.description: JSON string with extra context: for clicks/submissions `{"payload": {...form fields...}, "browser": {"address": ip, "user-agent": ua}}`; for send errors `{"error": "..."}`

ids.description: Result ids (`Result.id` strings) of the recipients to resend to

Company.description: An organization campaigns can be attributed to. `name_he` and `customer_id` are sourced from Hudu via `/companies/hudu`.

name_he.description: Hebrew display name (used in generated reports)

HuduCompany.description: A company as returned by the n8n hudu-companies webhook. Note the field names differ from `Company`: `nickname` is the Hebrew name and `id_number` is the customer id.

id.description: Hudu company id (not a Gophish id)

archived.description: Always `false` in responses — archived companies are filtered out

Domain.description: A sending/landing domain managed through the Domains page. DNS / M365 provisioning is performed by n8n, not by Gophish.

company_id.description: Owning Company (0 = unassigned). Used by the campaign wizard to auto-fill the listener URL and pick a matching sending profile.

ip.description: IP for the root A record

auto_a_record.description: Provision a root A record pointing at `ip`

configure_365.description: Provision the Microsoft 365 mail record set (MX, SPF, autodiscover, DKIM, DMARC)

records.description: Desired DNS record set — only populated in the `POST /domains/` response (not persisted)

DNSRecord.description: A single record in a domain's desired DNS record set (also part of the n8n webhook payload).

priority.description: MX priority (omitted for other record types)

Template.description: An email template. Subject, text and HTML support Go template variables (see `POST /templates/`).

envelope_sender.description: Optional SMTP MAIL FROM override; defaults to the sending profile's from_address

TemplateRequest.description: At least one of `text` / `html` is required.

Attachment.description: An email attachment. Text-based attachment types also support template variables.

html.description: Page HTML. Forms are rewritten on save to post back to the phishing server.

capture_credentials.description: Record submitted form fields (passwords excluded unless capture_passwords)

capture_passwords.description: Also record password fields — stored in **plaintext**

redirect_url.description: Where the victim is redirected after submitting

username.description: SMTP auth username (omit for unauthenticated relays)

from_address.description: Plain email address used as the From / envelope sender

ignore_cert_errors.description: Skip TLS certificate verification on the SMTP connection

headers.description: Custom headers added to every email (values support template variables)

User.description: A Gophish user account. Password hashes are never returned.

password_change_required.description: Force a password change at next login

account_locked.description: Locked accounts cannot log in; existing sessions are invalidated

UserRequest.description: Payload for creating/updating a user. On update, omit `password` to keep the current one.

password.description: Must satisfy the password policy (min 8 chars). Required on create.

Webhook.description: An outbound event webhook. Active webhooks receive a JSON POST for every campaign event, signed with `X-Gophish-Signature: sha256=<HMAC-SHA256 of body using secret>`.

IMAP.description: IMAP account polled for user-reported phishing emails. **Note:** `port` and `imap_freq` are JSON strings.

restrict_domain.description: Only process reports from this domain (empty = no restriction)

delete_reported_campaign_email.description: Delete matched campaign emails from the mailbox after processing

imap_freq.description: Polling interval in seconds, as a string

template.description: Template reference by name; omit for the built-in test message

smtp.description: Either `{"name": "..."}` referencing an existing profile, or a complete inline sending profile

url.description: Base URL used to render `{{.URL}}` in the test email
